text: I feel as though I am moving through contrasting spaces of thought—first, a quiet, almost contemplative emptiness, where bean bags and open floors invite daydreams and uncertainty. Then, I encounter a vibrant wall celebrating research and human-machine relationships, sparking my curiosity. There is a sense of purpose here, a pulse of progress and inquiry against the silence of the previous room. I find myself pondering how emptiness prepares the mind to absorb novelty, and how knowledge requires both solitude and engagement to flourish.
